title: Suttonia fauriei
intro: Suttonia fauriei, also known as Korean angelica tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, characterized by its large, compound leaves and umbrella-like inflorescences. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Suttonia fauriei include coumarins, volatile oils, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat colds, fever, and other respiratory ailments.
